subject: DevOps
title: Self-Service Infrastructure
description: A micro-lesson that makes Self-Service Infrastructure usable.

hook.text: A developer needed a new database. They filed a ticket. Three weeks later, ops created it. Three weeks for a database.

buildup.text: Ticket-driven infrastructure creates bottlenecks. Ops becomes a gatekeeper. Dev waits. Everyone suffers.

discovery.text: IaC modules are reusable templates. A developer picks a module, sets parameters, and runs it. Self-service.

twist.text: Guardrails baked into modules enforce standards. Developers get freedom. Platform teams keep control. Both win.

climax.text: Internal developer platforms wrap IaC in a UI or CLI. Create a database in five minutes, compliant by default.

punchline.text: IaC modules enable self-service. No tickets, no waiting, no drift.

quiz.question: How do IaC modules enable self-service infrastructure?
options: Developers use pre-built templates with guardrails instead of filing tickets
Modules automatically detect what infrastructure is needed
They eliminate the need for any platform team oversight
